title: Electronics enthusiast begins breadboard-based Intel 386 system build a large step up from their previous PC-XT and PC-AT breadboard projects
summary: Breadboarding Labs is crafting a retro Intel 386 PC build on solderless breadboards, marking a significant upgrade from previous PC-XT and PC-AT projects.
insight: Pushing microprocessor boundaries with innovative breadboarding techniques

title: Amazon, Microsoft, and Google under investor pressure to disclose site-specific data center water and power consumption more than a dozen shareholders ask for transparency ahead of annual investor meetings
summary: Investors pressure Amazon, Microsoft, and Google for data center water and energy consumption transparency.
insight: Companies' environmental impact affects long-term financial sustainability.

title: Intel's OEM-only Bartlett Lake CPU modded to run on consumer Z790 motherboard beats AMD's Ryzen 9 9900X3D in Cinebench multi-core test Core 9 273QPE has 12 cores, 24 threads, and hits 5.4GHz
summary: A modded Intel Bartlett Lake CPU outperforms AMD's Ryzen 9 9900X3D in Cinebench multi-core test, achieving around 33,000 points on a Z790 motherboard. This modified CPU boasts 12 Raptor Cove P-cores and reaches a 5.4GHz frequency. The results are comparable to the Core i7-14700.
insight: Intel's modified P-core CPU beats AMD in multi-core performance unexpectedly

title: US lawmakers aim to ban export of DUV chipmaking and etching tools to leading firms in China bipartisan proposal would ban lithography equipment for Huawei, SMIC, and others
summary: US lawmakers propose bipartisan ban on exporting DUV chipmaking tools to Chinese firms tied to China's military.
insight: Protecting US national security by limiting China's chip production capabilities.

title: Why TSMC grew four times faster than its foundry rivals in 2025 price hikes, vertical integration, and commanding technology lead pay dividends
summary: TSMC's significant growth can be attributed to its advantages in vertical integration, commanding technology lead, and price hikes. These factors have contributed to its substantial outperformance compared to its foundry rivals. The company's strong position is sustained over multiple product cycles.
insight: Vertical integration and tech lead drive TSMC's outsized success.

title: MSI MAG A1200PLS PCIE5 1200W power supply review: A competent 1200W unit, but pricey
summary: The MSI MAG A1200PLS PCIE5 offers strong efficiency and build quality, but is marred by a high price and thermal weakness under heavy loads.
insight: High price and thermal weakness limit its long-term reliability potential.

title: Get 32GB of Corsair Vengeance RAM for less than $200 when bundled with AMD's 9800X3D and X870-E motherboard free liquid cooler and a copy of Crimson Desert to kickstart a top AM5 gaming build
summary: Get a top AM5 gaming build with AMD's 9800X3D and Corsair Vengeance RAM for under $200. The bundle includes an X870-E motherboard, free liquid cooler, and a copy of Crimson Desert. This deal cuts costs and offers excellent performance.
insight: High-performance gaming build at a significantly reduced cost point

title: Creality Sermoon S1 Review: Accessible 3D Scanning
summary: The Creality Sermoon S1 offers improved 3D scanning capabilities at an accessible price. While not flawless, it's a step up from previous Creality scanners. However, users may still encounter some minor issues.
insight: Accessible 3D scanning for hobbyists and makers at an affordable price.

title: 80 different Microsoft Copilot products have been mapped out by expert, but there may be more than 100 'What happens when you name everything Copilot,' an AI consultant mapped out the myriad products
summary: An AI expert has mapped out 80 Microsoft Copilot products, but there may be more. A chart of these products has been created to track their various applications. This extensive list highlights the company's focus on AI-powered tools.
insight: Microsoft's AI strategy is rapidly expanding and diversifying product lines

title: Linux devs start removing support for 37-year-old Intel 486 CPU head honcho Linus Torvalds says 'zero real reason' to continue support
summary: Linux kernel developers have started to remove support for the Intel 486 CPU, citing a lack of relevance. Linus Torvalds stated that there's 'zero real reason' to continue supporting the 37-year-old processor. This move reflects the evolving nature of computing technology.
insight: Legacy support no longer aligns with modern computing requirements.

title: WD Innovation Day 2026 press Q&A transcript: roadmap plans to reach 60TB with ePMR and 100TB via HAMR by 2029 'at some point, the laws of physics will require us to transition to HAMR'
summary: WD leadership discusses roadmap plans for 60TB and 100TB storage targets by 2029, leveraging ePMR and HAMR technologies.
insight: Transitioning to HAMR will be necessary due to physics limitations

title: Nvidia's own DLSS 5 announcement video gets taken down by YouTube in Italy due to a copyright strike local TV channel sent a copyright strike to every YouTube video for using the trailer it used for its own broadcast
summary: YouTube removed Nvidia's DLSS 5 announcement video due to a copyright strike, affecting nearly every video containing the DLSS 5 trailer. The strike was initiated by a local TV channel for using the trailer in its own broadcast. YouTube's AI moderator acted errantly on the DMCA takedown.
insight: AI moderators can make mistakes affecting creators' content

title: NASA Artemis Watch 2.0 An ESP32-S3-powered, NASA-inspired wearable kit for education
summary: The NASA Artemis Watch 2.0 is a programmable, NASA-themed smartwatch based on ESP32-S3, featuring WiFi, Bluetooth, and various sensors.
insight: Empowering educational hands-on learning experiences with cutting-edge, space-inspired technology.

title: AAEON Intelli TWL01 Edge fanless mini PC features up to Core 3 N355 Twin Lake CPU for kiosks and digital signage applications
summary: The AAEON Intelli TWL01 Edge is a fanless mini PC designed for kiosks and digital signage. It features up to a Core 3 N355 Twin Lake CPU, 16GB of RAM, and dual 4K display support. The device offers various connectivity options and mounting possibilities.
insight: Industrial-grade mini PC optimizes digital signage and kiosk deployments

title: Artemis II sets record distance from Earth during Moon flyby
summary: Artemis II has achieved a record distance from Earth during its Moon flyby. Communications will be lost for nearly an hour as the Orion spacecraft passes behind the Moon. This milestone marks a significant step in deep space exploration.
insight: Breaking distance records paves way for manned Mars missions

title: Presenting the full class of Rising Stars 2026 honorees
summary: Fierce Sensors Rising Stars 2026 honors 32 distinguished individuals in the field. They will be introduced at Sensors Converge 2026 in Santa Clara, CA, May 5-7. This recognition celebrates innovation and excellence.
insight: Recognizing innovation and excellence in the sensors and technology field.

title: AI is now taking over game servers, and Stormgate is the first casualty
summary: Stormgate's multiplayer modes are going offline due to a game server outage. The outage is caused by Frost Giant's third-party provider being acquired by an AI company. Players will experience a planned outage.
insight: AI's growing influence threatens game server security and stability.

title: Russia's attempt to block VPNs is causing widespread banking outages
summary: Russia's attempts to control the internet are causing widespread banking outages, with users relying on VPNs to access banned services like Telegram. The Kremlin's efforts are disrupting financial transactions and daily life for millions of Russians. VPNs have become a crucial lifeline for those trying to circumvent censorship.
insight: Russia's internet control efforts are crippling financial systems nationwide.

title: Microsoft now has 80 different "Copilot" products, and counting
summary: Microsoft has 80 'Copilot' products, as counted by consultant Tey Bannerman, across its ecosystem.
insight: Microsoft's extensive Copilot offerings demonstrate AI's widespread integration in its services.

title: Windows 11 will be force-updated to version 25H2 using machine learning
summary: Windows 11 25H2 will be rolled out to devices running 24H2 editions before support ends.
insight: Forced updates ensure continued security after official support expiration.

title: Netflix's Void AI can remove objects from video and show how scenes evolve without them
summary: Netflix's Void AI can remove objects from video scenes and adjust for their absence.
insight: Precise object removal enhances video editing and analysis capabilities.

title: World's largest battery maker is taking EV tech to the seas
summary: The world's largest battery maker is expanding its electric technology to ships, equipping over 900 vessels with its batteries, mainly for coastal and port operations.
insight: Electrifying shipping reduces emissions and promotes sustainable maritime transportation

title: Samsung may add a "Pro" model to Galaxy S lineup, matching Apple's strategy
summary: Samsung may introduce a 'Pro' model to its Galaxy S lineup, slotting between the Plus and Ultra, inheriting Ultra's features.
insight: Samsung matches Apple's strategy with a new premium phone tier.

title: Target puts customers on the hook for AI shopping assistant errors
summary: Target introduces AI shopping assistant, prioritizing convenience over customer protection.
insight: Customers may be liable for AI errors in purchases and recommendations.

title: New 3D-printed microrobot mimics worm-like motion at microscopic scale
summary: A team of Dutch researchers created a 3D-printed microstructure that mimics worm-like motion. When exposed to an electric field, the structure propels itself forward. This self-sustaining motion is observed at the microscopic scale.
insight: Unlocking self-propulsion at the microscale for various biomedical applications

title: Florida school to deploy security drones designed to stop school shooters mid-attack
summary: A Florida school is deploying security drones to stop school shooters mid-attack, backed by state funding. The technology, developed by Mithril, aims to enhance school safety through rapid response. Pilots will operate the drones from a central headquarters.
insight: Rapid intervention technology hopes to reduce school shooter fatalities.

title: Samsung Messages is officially on the way out, pushing users to Google Messages
summary: Samsung Messages will be discontinued in July 2026, advising users to switch to Google Messages for a consistent Android experience.
insight: Samsung abandoning native messaging app impacts user choice and loyalty.

title: Japan's Rapidus ramps up 2nm chip plans while eyeing factories on the Moon
summary: Rapidus is advancing its 2nm chip production with pilot line operations.
insight: Japan leads the charge in developing cutting-edge semiconductor technology.

title: Iran threatens to "annihilate" Stargate AI data center backed by OpenAI and Nvidia
summary: Iran threatens retaliatory measures against US-backed IT facilities, including Stargate AI.
insight: US tech companies' foreign investment risks sparking global conflict escalation

title: Banks chasing SpaceX IPO were told by Elon Musk to buy Grok subscriptions
summary: Elon Musk is requiring banks to buy Grok subscriptions as a condition for advising on SpaceX's IPO. This includes spending tens of millions of dollars on the AI. Integration of the AI is already underway for some banks.
insight: Musk is leveraging the IPO to promote and profit from Grok
